title: Create durable advanced program packets for AMP201 through AMP262
persona: Security, operations, logistics, public-sector, industry, and executive reviewers
steps: Open representative AMP201-AMP262 workspaces from the generated Assistant subnav.
Review source signals, risk assessment, recommendations, artifact, approval route, and rollback plan.
expected: Each AMP201-AMP262 workspace persists an AssistantAdvancedProgramPacket row with a distinct ampNumber and programKey.
Each packet has a domain-specific nav label, title, artifact, approval owners, and no-mutation scope.
The shared advanced-program UI displays preview metrics, packet history, review notes, and queue-review controls.

No security, shared service, travel, fleet, yard, robotics, warehouse, freight, customs, supplier, procurement, grant, industry, public-sector, emergency, defense, space, maritime, rail, air cargo, or cross-industry autonomy records are mutated automatically.

title: Queue human review for AMP201 through AMP262 packets
steps: Create or select a DRAFT packet.
Add a review note describing the downstream decision.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and records actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
AssistantActionQueueItem uses an AMP-specific actionKind such as assistant_amp201_review or assistant_amp262_review.
AssistantAuditEvent states that downstream records were not changed automatically.

title: Cross-domain and industry packets preserve no-silent-mutation guardrails
steps: Open a packet with active risk signals.
Inspect recommendationJson, approvalPlanJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson.
Queue review without executing downstream changes.
expected: recommendationJson requires review before operational, security, regulated, or industry-network execution.
approvalPlanJson names the responsible owner groups.
rollbackPlanJson preserves source records and requires a fresh packet when evidence changes materially.
Autonomous industry network recommendations remain review-only and cannot self-expand autonomy.
